Schkuhria pinnata, also known as "Piney Greasewood" or "Greasewood", is a flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Schkuhria pinnata include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.

Sckuhria dichotoma is known to produce small, white or yellowish flowers, which are arranged in a compound umbel shape.
